title: bug(shell): `ctrl+c` in `podman machine ssh` shell on windows is acting weird
body:
### Issue Description

On windows, when running a ssh shell into a podman machine through `podman machine ssh`, using `ctrl+c` kinda exit the shell (I don't want that), but still not really exit it, and just get the terminal in a weird state.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. run `podman machine ssh`
2. type `ctrl+c`
3. assert weird behaviour



### Describe the results you received

Weird terminal behavour

### Describe the results you expected

The terminal should just return line / clean the line, not half exit the terminal

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/385fa323-7cd8-4847-a0fd-cef51ad322f4

### Additional information

comments.body: The "weird" behavior should be WSL-only and related to the [nested execution of systemd at machine boot](https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/17835).

> The "weird" behavior should be WSL-only and related to the https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/17835.

@l0rd not sure this is related to the nested execution, as using the wsl command which state 

> You will be automatically entered into a nested process namespace where
systemd is running. If you need to access the parent namespace, hit ctrl-d
or type exit. This also means to log out you need to exit twice.

Here is a demo

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f0db0e4-38da-477b-9d03-d761ae363dbe

Not sure what are the difference here

I am sorry, I thought that the weird behavior was that after `CTRL+C` you still had to exit to get back to PowerShell. But looking at it closer, after `CTRL+C` you get a PowerShell prompt right? And after `exit` you get back to the machine VM terminal? I am asking because I am not able to reproduce it:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e1214b48-6f42-42f5-bcf6-0a50e643d2e5

> after CTRL+C you get a PowerShell prompt right?  And after exit you get back to the machine VM terminal?

@l0rd yes, exactly, I see that you are using PowerShell 7.5.1, I tried updating it, no success.

I tried updating the Terminal windows application, same result.

I tried resetting all the parameters of the Terminal application, same result.

I tried removing the default `ctrl+c` binding for copying, same result

:/ I have no idea how to debug that, but this is very frustrating ahha

Please try with `--log-level=debug` so that we get more information. 


For example, this is the output on my side:

```log
> podman machine --log-level=debug ssh
time="2025-05-27T16:53:07+02:00" level=info msg="C:\\Users\\mario\\git\\podman\\bin\\windows\\podman.exe filtering at log level debug"
time="2025-05-27T16:53:07+02:00" level=debug msg="Using Podman machine with `wsl` virtualization provider"
time="2025-05-27T16:53:07+02:00" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\mario\\.config\\containers\\podman\\machine\\wsl is 52"
time="2025-05-27T16:53:07+02:00" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\mario\\.local\\share\\containers\\podman\\machine\\wsl is 57"
time="2025-05-27T16:53:07+02:00" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\mario\\.local\\share\\containers\\podman\\machine\\wsl\\cache is 63"
time="2025-05-27T16:53:07+02:00" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\mario\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\podman is 40"
time="2025-05-27T16:53:07+02:00" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\mario\\.config\\containers\\podman\\machine\\wsl\\podman-machine-default.json is 80"
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
time="2025-05-27T16:53:07+02:00" level=debug msg="Executing: ssh [-i C:\\Users\\mario\\.local\\share\\containers\\podman\\machine\\machine -p 49241 user@localhost -o IdentitiesOnly=yes -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=NUL -o CheckHostIP=no -o LogLevel=ERROR -o SetEnv=LC_ALL= -t]\n"
Last login: Tue May 27 15:54:00 2025 from ::1
[user@rascarcapac ~]$
```

And I can see the SSH command that is executed:

```
$MACHINE_FOLDER = "C:\\Users\\mario\\.local\\share\\containers\\podman\\machine\\machine"
ssh -i $MACHINE_FOLDER -p 49241 user@localhost -o IdentitiesOnly=yes -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=NUL -o CheckHostIP=no -o LogLevel=ERROR -o SetEnv=LC_ALL= -t
```

You can check if you get the same behavior using SSH rather than `podman machine ssh`. If it does, check your SSH config.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> Please try with --log-level=debug so that we get more information.

Don't seem to provide much more info

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df43da15-2c5e-4446-ba01-3ff587e0a3c9)

When using the SSH command directly to connect to the machine, no apparent issue, the `CTRL+c` has the indented efect

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/16fa828d-3e09-4f48-bb73-21520396305b)
